title: Retro adds upstream/downstream classification phase
description: Rejected as duplicate of retro-upstream-classifier (status: backlog). Both address routing retro findings to upstream (Momentum-level) vs project-local backlog with the same core mechanic (skills/agents/rules → upstream). Originating item: nornspun sprint-2026-04-12 retro PA-02. Additional user quote 'What goes upstream to momentum and what is an issue for nornspun?' and handoff-to-upstream-fix reference are marginal context deltas — existing story already captures multi-retro user-quote evidence pattern.

title: Composed file staleness detection policy
description: Shape the policy for when .claude/guidelines/agents/ files are considered stale and regenerated: stack change, guideline change, time-based, or manual only.

title: code-reviewer Withmartian replay fixture — investigate dataset access
description: Build a behavioral micro-eval fixture that replays the Withmartian 50-PR golden set against momentum:code-reviewer and verifies catch rate matches published frontier rates. SHAPING because we need to investigate Withmartian dataset licensing, access, and whether the 50-PR set is publicly available before committing to the fixture. If accessible, promote to ARTIFACT in a future triage. If not, look for an alternative public code-review benchmark (Macroscope, etc.). Source: triage — conversation, related to E3 candidate from the ForgeCode-style micro-eval design pass.

title: Downstream agent cold-context spawn contract
description: Scope how a spawned agent receives KB excerpts as cold context — what triggers the lookup, who runs the grep, how results are injected into the spawn prompt. Decide whether this belongs in impetus, kb-ingest, or a new skill.

title: dashboard-ux-wireframes stub — superseded by Claude Design Pass 4–7
description: Stub at _bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/stories/dashboard-ux-wireframes.md (derives_from: DEC-006). Seven Claude Design passes (Pass 1 density + badges, Pass 2 Level 2 drill-down, Pass 3 canvas expansion, Pass 4 hi-fi prototype, Pass 5 sprint + navigation, Pass 6 story detail + dark mode, Pass 7 reading mode) fully satisfy the hierarchical drill-down wireframes the stub asked for. DEC-011 captures the implementation foundations. Stub is retired.

title: feature-dependency-graph-ux-wireframes stub — going list-only, no graph view
description: Stub at _bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/stories/feature-dependency-graph-ux-wireframes.md. Decision in this triage: retire the graph mode pill and ship list-only deps in canvas Level-2. The graph view will not be designed. Stub is retired.

title: Empty state designs for canvas — handle ad hoc during dev
description: Surfaces lacking explicit empty-state designs: draft sprint (no stories), Flywheel with zero findings (fresh project), Features lens with zero features (new install), Level-2 with no stories assigned, Level-3 with empty dev notes. Decision: not a pre-implementation blocker for a solo-dev tool. Implementation will surface specific gaps naturally. Re-triage if dev encounters an empty state that needs more than improvisation.

title: Canvas Phase 1 builds against stub JSON — wire to .momentum/ when Impetus relocation lands
description: DEC-011 Phase 0 names the Impetus state relocation as a prerequisite for canvas Phase 1. Decision: don't hard-block — canvas Phase 1 scaffold reads from stub JSON files in dev, then wires to real .momentum/sprints/, .momentum/stories/, .momentum/signals/ paths when the Impetus stories land. The dependency note belongs in the Phase 1 story when create-story runs on it.

title: feature-status → momentum:canvas — hard rename, no shim
description: DEC-011 D1 captures the rename. Decision in this triage: hard rename, no backward-compat shim. Solo practice — no external consumers to break. Captured here so the Phase 1 canvas scaffold story includes the rename without re-debating shim vs hard.

title: Canvas Level-2 deps render list-only — drop the mode pill from Pass 5 design
description: Pass 5 Level-2 includes a DepGraphStub with mode pills (list / graph / direct-only) but only list mode was rendered. Decision in this triage: retire the pill, ship list-only deps. When the canvas Phase 5 (Level-2 feature detail) story is created, spec deps as list-only with no mode pill — do not implement the graph or direct-only modes.

title: __DATA__ injection contract between canvas skill and Vite bundle
description: DEC-011 D2 names the injection pattern (skill builds __DATA__ object, splices <script>window.__DATA__ = {...}</script> into the Vite-built template) but does not specify the schema or interface contract. Shape the contract before Phase 2 data plumbing to prevent rework across lens stories. May be absorbed into the Phase 1 create-story pass (spec the __DATA__ object shape) or elevated to a discrete exploration story before Phase 2 starts. Source: feature-breakdown:momentum-canvas.

title: Decide: iterative behavioral verifier inside dev (gan-style-harness gap)
description: Analysis paper at _bmad-output/research/analysis-momentum-verification-gaps-vs-gan-style-harness-2026-04-27.md surfaces a structural gap in Momentum verification: artifact validation (AVFL/code-reviewer/architecture-guard) and sprint-end behavioral validation (e2e-validator) are present, but no per-story per-iteration agent drives a live UI/system and feeds observations back to the dev agent. ECC ships gan-style-harness for this exact pattern (Generator + Evaluator-with-Playwright + iterative loop with file-based feedback), citing Anthropic March 2026 harness paper. Decision options: (A) new live-evaluator sub-agent inside dev with weighted runtime score convergence; (B) extend dev agent to self-verify via Playwright/cmux (no producer-judge split); (C) move e2e-validator earlier, per-story before merge; (D) reject per Anthropic's 'strip when models improve' principle and revisit when retro evidence justifies. Decision must land before any story is written — getting the boundary wrong creates redundant gates, double-runs, or evaluator drift into dev's job. Open questions: has retro data shown the cost of this gap? Could cmux-browser already cover the use case cheaply? Source: analysis paper §5.1, §7.1.

title: Sweep-verification AC for migration stories (enrich avfl-migration-story-exhaustive-call-site-audit)
description: Any 'all N consumers updated' AC must include a grep proving zero residual references. Consolidate with existing avfl-migration-story-exhaustive-call-site-audit and subtractive-story-consumer-audit.

title: Research primary-source verification (enrich research-prompts-are-date-anchored-with-primary-source-directives)
description: Add deadlink filter and gameable-metrics substitution rule (commits/contributor-distribution/downloads instead of stars) to existing research-prompts story.

title: momentum:release UX scope (enrich semver-v2-version-release-skill)
description: Existing semver-v2-version-release-skill should expand to cover: computes semver bump, updates plugin.json, commits, asks before push — full momentum:release UX.

title: Sprint-planning co-touch collision detection (enrich shared-file-contention-fix)
description: Detect overlapping story touches on shared workflow files during sprint-planning; emit reconciliation warning. Consolidate with shared-file-contention-fix.

title: Sprint-close worktree hygiene (enrich sprint-dev-worktree-branch-cleanup-gate)
description: Add periodic stale-worktree/branch cleanup at sprint complete. Existing sprint-dev-worktree-branch-cleanup-gate covers Phase 7 cleanup — extend or add sprint-close routine.

title: Spawn-prompt parallelism examples (enrich enforce-parallel-spawning-for-independent-subagents)
description: Add example-driven parallelism guidance to highest-volume spawn prompts. p50 tool-results/assistant-turns ratio was 0.72 — no agent averaged >1 parallel call per turn.

failure_diagnosis.didnt_work: agents dont apply parallelism without concrete examples in spawn prompts
failure_diagnosis.learned: highest-volume spawn prompts need inline parallel examples to shift behavior

title: Zero-turn agent investigation (enrich agent-spawn-observability-metric)
description: 2 zero-turn subagents detected in sprint. Investigate spawn conditions. Existing agent-spawn-observability-metric tracks zero-turn as waste signal — add investigation path.

title: Codify handoff location convention (.momentum/handoffs/) and propagate cross-project
description: Handoff written to wrong directory; developer had to reason convention from scratch. Small-scope rule addition to CLAUDE.md and relevant skills.

title: Decision skill auto-creates backlog stubs for implementation-implying decisions
description: Decisions that imply implementation work were not turned into backlog stubs, creating a decision-to-implementation gap. Decision skill should prompt for stub creation.

title: Investigate zero-turn dev-fixer anomaly (1218KB agent, 0 assistant turns)
description: L98 dev-fixer agent recorded as 1218KB with 0 assistant turns. Unclear if parse failure or genuine zero-output. Needs diagnosis.

title: Tag agent runs by purpose (sprint-story/benchmark/ingest/exploratory)
description: 25-30% of sprint agent-effort was benchmark/ingest work commingled with real story work, obscuring sprint throughput metrics. Needs purpose tagging in transcript metadata and transcript-query filtering.

title: Document single-turn consolidator pattern as canonical in AVFL/team-review skills
description: Single-turn consolidators work correctly but may be misidentified as abandoned agents in audits. Pattern should be documented so auditors recognize it.

title: Document AVFL post-merge one-iteration convergence as canonical success case
description: Quick-fix AVFL loop converged in 6 minutes / 1 iteration. Success pattern worth documenting in sprint-dev Phase 7 as the target behavior.

title: Bulk-approve UX: prompt to split for batches above N items
description: Developer reversed bulk A&A&A approval to insist on splitting first. UX risk when bulk-approve flow lacks a split-first prompt.

title: Audit skill descriptions for routing distinctiveness
description: Developer redirected agent to correct skill for grooming task. Impetus routing may misfire if skill descriptions have overlapping trigger phrases.

title: Track errors-per-turn ratio as a decomposition signal in audits
description: High error concentration in top-turn agents signals scope creep. A threshold metric (>2 errors/10 turns) should trigger investigation and decomposition recommendation in retro auditor-execution.

title: Audit other skills for TeamCreate misuse per spawning-patterns.md
description: TeamCreate was misused in retro skill (now fixed). Other skills may have the same violation. Full audit of skill corpus against spawning-patterns.md fan-out vs TeamCreate decision rule.

title: Reconcile Tier 2 framing: constitution-builder/SKILL.md vs DEC-001/DEC-008
description: constitution-builder/SKILL.md:14-18 describes the three tiers as Cold KB / Hot Constitution / Hot Selective, but DEC-001 and DEC-008 D1 name them Tier 1 (Hot Constitution) / Tier 2 (Composed Specialist Agent File) / Tier 3 (Cold KB). The SKILL.md framing conflates Tier 2 with a runtime retrieval pattern (symptom -> wiki-query pull-in), which is not what Tier 2 is per the decisions. Fix: update constitution-builder/SKILL.md to use the canonical Tier 1/2/3 numbering and call the symptom-driven pull-in by its actual name (runtime retrieval pattern, not a tier). Verify the same numbering is used consistently across all skills that reference the architecture.

title: Rework constitution-builder to generate project domain knowledge only (not routing)
description: constitution-builder currently generates ## Permissions + ## Standing Rules + ## Quick Routing for agent skills. Per architectural clarification session 2026-05-14: the constitution is project-wide shared knowledge (same for every agent), so it should contain ONLY project domain knowledge — a mix of embedded facts and KB lookups for project-specific context. Routing entries (symptom → wiki-query) are agent-specific and belong in the agent manifesto, not the constitution. Rework constitution-builder to: (1) generate project domain knowledge sections (embedded facts + KB-sourced context), (2) remove Quick Routing generation — that moves to a new manifesto-builder skill. Permissions and Standing Rules that are truly cross-cutting (apply to every agent) stay; agent-specific permissions move to manifesto-builder.

title: New manifesto-builder skill — per-agent routing and agent-specific permissions
description: Per architectural clarification session 2026-05-14: each agent needs its own manifesto (agent-specific differentiation). The manifesto-builder is a new skill that generates per-agent content: (1) agent-specific Quick Routing table (symptom → wiki-query mappings for this role × domain), (2) agent-specific Permissions (path patterns + tool restrictions for this agent's scope). build-guidelines orchestrates manifesto-builder once per role × domain pair to produce each agent's manifesto section, then merges it with the base body and shared constitution to produce the final composed Tier 2 agent file. Pairs with the reworked constitution-builder (iq-20260514-constitution-builder-rework-domain-knowledge-only).

title: code-reviewer-agent-definition — superseded by DEC-020/DEC-024
description: Not a base body. code-reviewer is a skill-applied constraint (context:fork + read-only tools). Separate agent definition file not needed. Superseded by DEC-020 D2 and DEC-024.

title: architect-guard-agent-definition — superseded by DEC-020/DEC-024
description: Not a base body. architecture-guard is a skill-applied constraint. Separate agent definition file not needed. Superseded by DEC-020 D2 and DEC-024.

title: documenter-agent-definition — superseded by DEC-020
description: Retro synthesizer is an inline spawn writer, not a typed role with its own base body. DEC-020 D3.

title: dev-fixer-agent-definition — superseded by DEC-020/DEC-025
description: Fixer is a constraint pattern (document owner + fix constraint injected by orchestrator). Not a base body. DEC-020 D4, DEC-025.

title: agents-md-manifest-format — superseded by DEC-023
description: Replaced entirely by momentum/agents.json routing table with machine-readable defaults and project entries. DEC-023.

title: build-guidelines-skill — superseded by DEC-026
description: Renamed to build-agents with redesigned scope as orchestrator of constitution-builder and agent-builder. Old story scope obsolete. DEC-026 D2.

title: manifesto-builder-new-skill — pending reconciliation with agent-builder (DEC-026)
description: Pre-DEC-026 proposal for manifesto-builder generating per-agent routing and permissions. DEC-026 D4 routes this concern to agent-builder-skill instead. Shape: reconcile after agent-builder-skill story is created. May be subsumed by agent-builder or remain distinct as a generation sub-step.

title: DEC-029: refine/rescope 7 existing backlog stories superseded by method-routed validation
description: DEC-029 (method-routed acceptance validation pipeline, 2026-05-17) supersedes or rescopes 7 existing backlog stories. Do NOT recreate as new stories — flag for the next momentum:refine pass against DEC-029. (1) acceptance-testing-process-and-standards: standard retired by D7, folds into the new enforced verification rule. (2) change-type-routing-in-sprint-dev: extends to verification-method routing per D5. e2e-validator cluster likely superseded/rescoped by the harness-driven e2e-validator rewrite: (3) e2e-validator-black-box-hardening, (4) e2e-validator-toolsearch-fix, (5) e2e-and-qa-validator-prompts-branch-standalone-vs-team, (6) e2e-client-side-coverage, (7) e2e-validator-fixture-spec-sync-stale-reference-detection. Phase 1 build gated on routing-table-schema-and-implementation landing momentum/agents.json (DEC-029 Gate 1). Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/decisions/dec-029-method-routed-acceptance-validation-pipeline-2026-05-17.md

title: momentum-model-routing-strategy: hardcoded model pins caused billing gate incidents twice
description: 23+ skill frontmatter files pin model: claude-sonnet-4-6, overriding the session's model selection. Developer hit the 1M billing gate in two separate sprint sessions as a direct result. Fix stub created.

feature_state_transition.evidence: Billing gate hit twice in sprint-2026-05-16; 23+ skill files hardcode claude-sonnet-4-6 in frontmatter, model-routing.md is a placeholder stub with no routing rules

failure_diagnosis.attempted: Running sprint workflows under 1M context while skill frontmatter pins model: claude-sonnet-4-6
failure_diagnosis.didnt_work: Hardcoded model pins conflict with session context tier, triggering billing gate on every subagent spawn
failure_diagnosis.learned: Skill frontmatter must never hardcode specific model IDs — model routing must be delegated to session-level config or declared via capability tier, not string pin

title: momentum-sprint-planning-to-ready: two defects found in sprint-2026-05-16
description: Sprint-planning workflow has two concurrent bugs: (1) continuous execution broken by inter-step confirmation gates and /model switch resetting state; (2) stale CLI subcommands and wrong skill namespaces. Fix stubs created for both.

feature_state_transition.evidence: Orchestrator halted twice for inter-step confirmations; /model switch reset execution state; sprint-current and sprint-stories CLI refs stale; bare avfl/create-story skill namespaces used instead of momentum: prefix

title: momentum-practice-distillation: two trust-model defects found in sprint-2026-05-16
description: Distill workflow missing (1) batch-approval context propagation from triage — 4 subagents broke the batch-approval promise; (2) .claude/rules/ write permission — distill blocked on new rule file creation. Fix stubs created.

feature_state_transition.evidence: 4 distill subagents paused on approval gates after batch approval was granted at triage; .claude/rules/handoff-conventions.md was not created until a subsequent session due to missing write permission

title: Parallel distill agents collide on shared practice files
description: When multiple distill agents are spawned in parallel targeting the same file (e.g., SKILL.md), git index.lock contention and content collisions cause 3-error recovery loops and 46-turn remediation. Serialization story created.

failure_diagnosis.attempted: Spawning multiple distill agents in parallel for efficiency
failure_diagnosis.didnt_work: Parallel agents targeting the same file collide on git index.lock and overwrite each other's changes, requiring multi-turn error recovery
failure_diagnosis.learned: Distill agents must be serialized per target file path — either queue them or enforce exclusive write authority per file in the distill orchestrator

title: Decide: iterative behavioral verifier inside dev (gan-style-harness gap)
description: Superseded by DEC-029 D4: AVFL extended with E2E lens (post-merge batch, PASS-sticky, fixer-routed) + D9 three-tier pipeline placing E2E after AVFL. Decision made. Source: original iq-20260428000000-7a3c9f12.

title: agents.json defaults block missing architect, pm, sm roles
description: Any skill calling agent-resolve --role architect/pm/sm errors at runtime. routing-table story did not enumerate these roles as required defaults.

title: gate and decision artifacts human-legibility requirement
description: Two artifacts this sprint flagged as incomprehensible. Gate and decision output written for LLM consumption, not human decision-making.

title: sprint-planning precondition enforcement — triage gate and dedup pass
description: Two required gates bypassed with no detection this sprint. Developer manually enforcing process sequencing.

title: decision ratification comprehension-alignment gate
description: Dispatcher architectural model misalignment discovered after significant research investment. Proposed design contradicted developer intent.

title: agent-builder approval gate absent from workflow.md
description: Phase 4 writes composed agent and routing entry unconditionally — no developer review gate. DoD item not implemented.

title: e2e-validator hardcoded service assumptions block all signal for practice projects
description: Third consecutive sprint with zero E2E signal. Service-stack assumptions permanently neutralize the gate for pure practice sprints.

title: sprint-dev fan-out heartbeat output
description: Developer blind for 18+ minutes during every fan-out. No progress signals emitted while parallel agents run.

title: decision ratification comprehension-alignment gate
description: Dispatcher architectural model misalignment discovered after significant research investment. Proposed design contradicted developer intent.

title: sprint-dev idempotent agent spawn with completion detection
description: 34 turns wasted per socket abort; two incidents this sprint. No deduplication guard in the spawning layer.

title: story DoD add hook smoke-test acceptance criterion
description: Hook deployed with invalid --no-git-ops flag; no pipeline validation caught it before session startup.

title: sprint-manager version-on-release enforcement at sprint close
description: Rule exists in .claude/rules/version-on-release.md but not consulted at close. Developer had to prompt version bump.

title: momentum:research session checkpoint and resume
description: Session terminated without recovery path. Developer had to manually re-orient agent to continue Hermes research.

title: AVFL structural lens add process-doc anti-pattern detector
description: Recurring violation despite memory entry. No quality gate catches agents defaulting to documentation over enforced rules.

title: triage and retro context-window monitoring with auto-handoff
description: Context exhaustion required manual model switch and handoff mid-triage. Developer absorbed the orchestration cost.

title: analyst-base-body tool contract — remove Write/Edit add ToolSearch
description: Write/Edit access violates read-only role contract defined in DEC-020. ToolSearch absent despite being needed.

title: ux-base-body output envelope — UX_OUTPUT_START to AGENT_OUTPUT_START
description: Sprint-dev orchestrators will fail parsing ux agent output. Cross-story integration failure not caught by story-level AVFL.

title: CREED blocks and eval files for analyst researcher ux base bodies
description: Behavioral identity unimplemented and no regression protection for three new agent roles. Carry-forward from sprint.

title: AVFL consolidator require file-existence check before dismissing structural path findings
description: Valid file-path finding dismissed; beads-dual-write-spike path discrepancy persists as latent inconsistency.

title: AVFL skip logging with developer reason capture
description: Developer-directed skips invisible to future retros. Practice has no audit trail for skipped validation steps.

title: momentum:research scope-alignment confirmation gate
description: Research scope narrower than intended; developer had to re-frame after research completed. Scope misalignment not caught at start.

title: browser-driver agent circuit breaker after N consecutive identical failures
description: 119 turns of wasted compute with repeated identical JS errors. No stopping condition for broken tool environments.

title: research and decision cost-and-licensing review step
description: Licensing implications not surfaced proactively for proposed beads/SDK integrations. Developer had to ask proactively.

title: subagent spawn pre-flight context tier check
description: 4 intake agents failed instantly on 1M billing gate. Zero work produced from entire intake wave due to context tier mismatch between parent and subagents.

title: sprint-manager autonomous closure checklist with terminal signal
description: Developer prompted for 4 separate closure steps (branch delete, remote cleanup, push, version bump). Practice does not emit a definitive sprint-closed signal.
